headline: Agent orchestration and security dominate this cycle, with educators building meta-harnesses to coordinate coding models while warning that production agents leak data and silently fail.
body: The recommended channels converge on orchestrating and securing agentic systems: Cole Medin demos Omnigent, a meta-harness that routes tasks across Claude Code, Codex, and Pi as interchangeable workers with human-in-the-loop governance and cross-device session persistence [4], while Cyril Imhof covers Claude Fable 5 with harness engineering and AI loops for regulated industries [2]. On the security side, IBM Technology examines whether AI agents should manage passwords against a record Patch Tuesday of over 200 CVEs [1] and presents Kagenti's identity-based, delegation-chain approach to preventing invisible breaches in multi-agent systems [3], plus an overview of AI pair programming as a debugging and code-review teammate [5]. Supporting videos add concrete reliability and tooling angles: Bright Data shows agents falsely claiming web access while hitting CAPTCHAs or stale data [11], Jack Roberts maps a seven-level Hermes agent progression and covers Microsoft Scout acting proactively across Teams, email, and calendar [6][8], and MiniMax M3 ships open weights with 1-million-token context and Anthropic- and OpenAI-compatible APIs [13]. Better Stack rounds it out with Voicebox, a local open-source voice studio positioned against ElevenLabs [0].

cited_items.title: Omnigent: The New Meta-Harness for EVERY Coding Agent - Claude Code, Codex, Pi, More
Claude Fable 5 with Harness Engineering & Loops Will Do Everything.
